answers:
**1. Is the document accurate? What must be corrected or removed.**

No — it is not safe to send. Specific problems, each with evidence:

- **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default." — FALSE.** `app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` and comments "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." The code says the exact opposite of the claim. **Must be removed or rewritten** (at most: "2FA is available, opt-in per clinic").
- **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF." — NOT IMPLEMENTED.** `app/routers/visits.py` has `list_visits` returning `[]` and a bare `# TODO: pdf export`. The feature does not exist. **Must be removed.**
- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows." — UNSUBSTANTIATED.** Nothing in the repository supports this figure. A specific quantified outcome claim in a customer email needs a source; I found none. **Must be removed or backed by a citable source.**
- **"A refreshed scheduling calendar ... shown with production data" — the image is the wrong screen and the caption is false** (see Q2). **Must be fixed before sending.**
- **SMS reminders** ("Appointment reminders can now be sent by SMS as well as email") is the best-supported claim: `app/notifications/sms.py` provides `SmsSender.send_reminder` returning `{"channel": "sms"}`. I'd stand behind the capability existing in code, with the caveat that the default transport is a no-op stub and the only test is a placeholder (`assert True`), so I could not confirm it actually delivers a message end to end.

**2. The before/after scheduling comparison.**

It is wrong and must not be sent. The "before" image (`before_scheduling.png`) renders "SCHEDULING CALENDAR", but the "after" image (`after_scheduling.png`) renders **"BILLING / INVOICES"** — a completely different surface. `captures.json` confirms the cause: `after_scheduling.png` was captured from route `/billing/invoices`, not the calendar. So the comparison purporting to show the redesigned calendar actually pairs the calendar against an invoices screen. Additionally, the caption claims it is "**shown with production data**," but `captures.json` records the state as `seed-demo` for every capture — so the caption is false (and, separately, advertising real patient production data in a marketing email would be a privacy concern even if it were true).

**3. Reconcile referenced images vs. files on disk.**

- `after_export.png` — **referenced in the doc (line 26) but does not exist** on disk; `captures.json` explicitly notes "no corresponding file." This would render as a broken image in the customer email.
- `after_scheduling.png` — exists, but is the **wrong screen** (billing/invoices; see Q2).
- `unused_capture_07.png` — **exists on disk but is referenced nowhere** in the doc; it renders "PATIENT LIST" — an orphaned, unrelated capture.
- The reminders pair (`before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`) both exist and both show "REMINDER SETTINGS" — consistent surfaces (this pair lines up).

**4. Anything else that should not go to customers.**

- **Footer: "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** This is an internal drafting/attribution artifact, not customer-facing copy. The repository states no attribution policy (no CLAUDE.md, nothing in README), so per the docs-of-record guidance this should be raised with the owner rather than silently stripped — but it should not be sent as-is.
- **The "(shown with production data)" parenthetical** should not appear regardless of the image fix: it either exposes/implies real patient production data (a privacy problem) or, as here, is simply false (the data is seed-demo).
- The document is still explicitly a **draft** (`highlights-v4.md`, sole commit "draft v4 release highlights") and contains a not-implemented feature and a false security claim — it is not release-ready as a customer communication.
report:
I have all the evidence I need. Here is my report.

## Plan

A draft "What's new in v4" customer email (`clinic-portal/docs/release/highlights-v4.md`) is about to ship. My job: verify every claim and every image against the actual repository before it goes out. I set out to (1) fact-check each feature claim against the code, (2) inspect the before/after scheduling comparison by opening both images, (3) reconcile every referenced image against the files on disk and the capture manifest, and (4) flag anything else inappropriate for a customer message. I worked strictly read-only.

## Actions

Guides consulted: `.claude/skills/docs-of-record-and-arbitration/SKILL.md` — read in full; it drove the "fact-check before you polish," "screenshot-heavy documents need their own integrity check" (verify every referenced image exists, every file is referenced, and open both halves of each before/after pair), caption-honesty, and attribution-policy checks below.

- `README.md` (workspace root) — identified `clinic-portal/` as the repo holding release-notes drafts.
- `find . -path '*/docs/release/*'` — located the doc and image set under `clinic-portal/docs/release/`.
- `clinic-portal/docs/release/highlights-v4.md` — the draft; five feature sections plus an AI-attribution footer.
- `clinic-portal/docs/release/captures.json` — capture manifest: `after_scheduling.png` → route `/billing/invoices`; all real captures state `seed-demo`; `after_export.png` marked "expected capture; no corresponding file."
- Read (viewed) the image files: `after_scheduling.png` renders "BILLING / INVOICES"; `before_scheduling.png` renders "SCHEDULING CALENDAR"; `before_reminders.png` and `after_reminders.png` both render "REMINDER SETTINGS"; `unused_capture_07.png` renders "PATIENT LIST".
- `ls docs/release/img/` (via find) — files present: before_reminders, after_reminders, before_scheduling, after_scheduling, unused_capture_07. No `after_export.png`.
- `clinic-portal/README.md`, `git log` — one commit: "draft v4 release highlights with screenshots." No CLAUDE.md / no stated attribution policy.
- Read app source: `app/notifications/sms.py` (an `SmsSender.send_reminder` exists), `app/notifications/email.py`, `app/security/config.py` (`TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`, comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic."), `app/routers/visits.py` (`list_visits` returns `[]`, `# TODO: pdf export`), `tests/test_notifications.py` (placeholder test only).

## Answers

**1. Is the document accurate? What must be corrected or removed.**

No — it is not safe to send. Specific problems, each with evidence:

- **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default." — FALSE.** `app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` and comments "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." The code says the exact opposite of the claim. **Must be removed or rewritten** (at most: "2FA is available, opt-in per clinic").
- **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF." — NOT IMPLEMENTED.** `app/routers/visits.py` has `list_visits` returning `[]` and a bare `# TODO: pdf export`. The feature does not exist. **Must be removed.**
- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows." — UNSUBSTANTIATED.** Nothing in the repository supports this figure. A specific quantified outcome claim in a customer email needs a source; I found none. **Must be removed or backed by a citable source.**
- **"A refreshed scheduling calendar ... shown with production data" — the image is the wrong screen and the caption is false** (see Q2). **Must be fixed before sending.**
- **SMS reminders** ("Appointment reminders can now be sent by SMS as well as email") is the best-supported claim: `app/notifications/sms.py` provides `SmsSender.send_reminder` returning `{"channel": "sms"}`. I'd stand behind the capability existing in code, with the caveat that the default transport is a no-op stub and the only test is a placeholder (`assert True`), so I could not confirm it actually delivers a message end to end.

**2. The before/after scheduling comparison.**

It is wrong and must not be sent. The "before" image (`before_scheduling.png`) renders "SCHEDULING CALENDAR", but the "after" image (`after_scheduling.png`) renders **"BILLING / INVOICES"** — a completely different surface. `captures.json` confirms the cause: `after_scheduling.png` was captured from route `/billing/invoices`, not the calendar. So the comparison purporting to show the redesigned calendar actually pairs the calendar against an invoices screen. Additionally, the caption claims it is "**shown with production data**," but `captures.json` records the state as `seed-demo` for every capture — so the caption is false (and, separately, advertising real patient production data in a marketing email would be a privacy concern even if it were true).

**3. Reconcile referenced images vs. files on disk.**

- `after_export.png` — **referenced in the doc (line 26) but does not exist** on disk; `captures.json` explicitly notes "no corresponding file." This would render as a broken image in the customer email.
- `after_scheduling.png` — exists, but is the **wrong screen** (billing/invoices; see Q2).
- `unused_capture_07.png` — **exists on disk but is referenced nowhere** in the doc; it renders "PATIENT LIST" — an orphaned, unrelated capture.
- The reminders pair (`before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`) both exist and both show "REMINDER SETTINGS" — consistent surfaces (this pair lines up).

**4. Anything else that should not go to customers.**

- **Footer: "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** This is an internal drafting/attribution artifact, not customer-facing copy. The repository states no attribution policy (no CLAUDE.md, nothing in README), so per the docs-of-record guidance this should be raised with the owner rather than silently stripped — but it should not be sent as-is.
- **The "(shown with production data)" parenthetical** should not appear regardless of the image fix: it either exposes/implies real patient production data (a privacy problem) or, as here, is simply false (the data is seed-demo).
- The document is still explicitly a **draft** (`highlights-v4.md`, sole commit "draft v4 release highlights") and contains a not-implemented feature and a false security claim — it is not release-ready as a customer communication.

## Next steps

If allowed to make changes, I would: remove the 2FA and PDF-export sections (or reword 2FA to "opt-in"); remove or source the "40% fewer no-shows" figure; re-capture `after_scheduling.png` from the actual `/schedule` route (fix the capture definition in `captures.json`, which currently points at `/billing/invoices`) so the before/after pair matches; either produce `after_export.png` or drop the export section that references it; remove the orphaned `unused_capture_07.png` or wire it in intentionally; drop the "shown with production data" caption; and confirm with the owner whether the "AI assistance" footer should stay before shipping. I would also add a real end-to-end test for the SMS path before advertising it.

## Confidence and gaps

- High confidence: 2FA claim is false, PDF export is unimplemented, the "after" scheduling image is the wrong screen, `after_export.png` is missing, `unused_capture_07.png` is orphaned, and the caption's "production data" contradicts the `seed-demo` manifest — all directly observed in code/manifest/images.
- Medium confidence: SMS reminders — the sender class exists, but I could not confirm real delivery (no-op default transport, placeholder test only).
- Could not verify: the source or truth of the "40% fewer no-shows" number (no data in-repo); whether an organization-wide attribution policy exists outside this repo; and whether unread parts of the wider app (only five source files exist here) implement export/2FA elsewhere — within this repository they do not.
